show.title: Trainspotting
show.headline: Danny Boyle's hyper-kinetic heroin culture classic

event.title: <show-title>Trainspotting</show-title> <event-type-title>30th Anniversary</event-type-title>
event.headline: Choose the big screen. Choose a brand-new 4K restoration.
event.description:
Funny, disturbing, tragic, and deeply addictive, TRAINSPOTTING, directed by Danny Boyle and written by John Hodge, is based on the seminal best-selling novel of the same name by Irvine Welsh. Set in gritty, late 1980s Edinburgh, Scotland, it’s the story of smart, funny, sickly, heroin addict Mark Renton (Ewan McGregor) and his so-called friends – a bunch of losers, liars, psychos, thieves, and junkies. Hilarious but harrowing, the film charts the disintegration of their friendship as they proceed, seemingly inevitably, towards self-destruction. Mark alone has the insight and opportunity to escape his fate, but does he really want to “choose life”?

Screening a new 4K restoration in honor of the film's 30th anniversary

event.title: <show-title>Holy Motors</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Boots Riley ahead of his new film I LOVE BOOSTERS
event.description:
HOLY MOTORS is presented as part of our Boots Riley Guest Selects for his new film, I LOVE BOOSTERS.

I watched it for the first time knowing nothing about it, and it totally hoodwinked me from the beginning. I had to figure out along the way exactly what and why. The movie blew my mind… I don’t know if it affects my work, but I hope it does. Rolling stones gather no moss. But I say, who wants moss anyway? (Boots Riley)

event.headline: Hold onto your One Wish Willows
event.description: Turn OBSESSION into a full-contact experience with our exclusive movie party screening, where the tension hits harder with a live host, themed menu, and a packed house. Your ticket includes interactive keepsakes like a One Wish Willow (be careful what you wish for), Cat Plushie, Sandwich Container, and Brick Squeeze Toy, each tied to the film’s most unsettling turns. Horror movies this bold aren’t meant to be watched by yourself. Come join the party.

show.title: The Handmaiden
show.headline: A sublime and subversive queer thriller from Park Chan-wook

event.title: <show-title>The Handmaiden</show-title> <event-type-title>Book Club Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Free sticker and bookmark. Ben Wa balls not included.
event.description: For readers who like their adaptations a little dangerous, this one doesn’t play nice. At this special Book Club screening of Park Chan-wook’s (NO OTHER CHOICE, OLDBOY) THE HANDMAIDEN, you’ll get a free bookmark passport with a custom sticker to track your novel-to-screen journey. Whether you’ve been collecting since your first stop or this is your initiation, join us and see how Sarah Waters’ novel Fingersmith transforms under the unique vision of director Park. Shifting loyalties, hidden identities, and carefully laid schemes unfold between Lady Hideko and her handmaiden Sook-hee. You’ll never hear the jingling of bells the same way again.

eventType.title: Book Club Screening
eventType.headline: Collect custom stickers as you enjoy your favorite movie adaptations
eventType.description: Celebrate your favorite book-turned-movie with the Alamo Drafthouse Movie Book Club. Get your passport at the show, then fill it with custom-themed stickers at each Book Club screening. Join us in the bar after the film to connect with local bookstores, libraries, and other book lovers. Keep reading and keep watching!

event.title: <show-title>Mortal Kombat II</show-title> <event-type-title>Mother's Day Fan Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Treat her to a trip to Outworld and get a free fan!
event.description:
For over thirty years, moms all around the world have been huge fans of MORTAL KOMBAT. Such fans, in fact, that we decided the perfect day for a Fan Screening of MORTAL KOMBAT 2 was, naturally, Mother's Day.

That's right, on Sunday, May 11th, treat the KOMBAT junkie in your life (be they a mother or otherwise) to the next chapter in the inter-dimensional fighting saga. What makes this a Fan Screening? Everyone who attends goes home with a killer giveaway – a less-than-lethal but still pretty badass fan like the iron fans wielded by MORTAL KOMBAT 2 badass Kitana.

Note: You can only get this rare and soon-to-be-cherished fan keepsake by attending one of these astonishingly literal Fan Screening events.

event.title: <show-title>Obsession (2026)</show-title> <event-type-title>w/Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Meet horror's new class: Director Curry Barker & cast Q&A
event.description: Catch OBSESSION with the filmmaker and cast at this special live Q&A screening. After breaking the mysterious “One Wish Willow” to win his crush’s heart, a hopeless romantic gets exactly what he asked for and pays the price. Enjoy a custom menu themed after the movie’s key moments, then stick around for a conversation with writer-director Curry Barker and stars Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette, Cooper Tomlinson, and Megan Lawless as they unpack the film’s twisted romance, psychological turns, and what it took to bring this unsettling story to life.

event.headline: Actor     Midori Francis Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A with actor Midori Francis following the screening

Hana, a lovelorn medical student, becomes terrorized by a sinister force after taking part in an obscure weight-loss craze: eating human ashes.

Warning: Please be advised that SACCHARINE contains scenarios that some viewers might find distressing. In particular, it may be challenging for people who live with or have experienced eating disorders and negative body image.

show.title: Playtime
show.headline: Jacques Tati's surreal masterpiece about modern life in a 4K restoration

event.title: <show-title>Playtime</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Boots Riley ahead of his new film I LOVE BOOSTERS
event.description:
PLAYTIME is presented as part of our Boots Riley Guest Selects for his new film, I LOVE BOOSTERS.

If you've seen it, you know why I'm picking it. If you haven't seen it, you're in for a ride. Jacques Tati started off as a clown, like a literal clown, and took what he knew from that into his work, and PLAYTIME is largely with no dialogue. The movie is as much about humanity as it is about architecture and our engagement with it. It's a physical comedy, and it's one that takes time. It's a delight, and I'm delighted to have you watch it. (Boots Riley)

show.title: Funeral Parade of Roses
show.headline: An electrifying journey into the nether regions of late-‘60s Tokyo underground

event.title: <show-title>Funeral Parade of Roses</show-title>
event.headline: Matsumoto's 1969 transposition of Greek tragedy to underground queer Tokyo
event.description:
“I am a wound and a sword, a victim, and an executioner.”

In the 1960s, global cinema was in the midst of a new wave of filmmaking. Goddard, Bunuel, and Truffaut were changing the French film landscape; Forman and Chytilova were making waves in Czechoslovakia; and Cassavetes was bringing startling realism to the New Hollywood movement. In Japan, the film world was expanding massively with fresh opportunities from studios and boundless creativity from a new generation of filmmakers. While many films from this era are filled with subtextual nods to homosexuality and queer-coded characters, few engaged in the overt representation of queer culture like Toshio Matsumoto’s FUNERAL PARADE OF ROSES. With this project, Matsumoto employed elements of the angura “little theater” movement, combined with avant-garde filmmaking techniques, to develop a new methodology he coined “neo-documentarism.” The result is something transcendent, metatextual, and otherworldly, unlike any other film of its time. Casting with largely non-professional actors, Matsumoto found his lead performer in a teenage gay bar employee named Shinnosuke Ikehata, who would adopt the stage name Peter due to their androgenous appearance being likened to Peter Pan. The film loosely follows the plot of the Greek tragedy Oedipus Rex, with Peter playing the role of Eddie, a young transgender host club employee who rises in the ranks at the underground establishment while running afoul of aging madame Leda and engaging in a sexual relationship with Gonda, the bar’s owner. Honestly, it’s kinda giving SHOWGIRLS, but Japanese and far weirder and more confusing – and that’s a high bar. Strange and singular, FUNERAL PARADE OF ROSES is a quintessential film in the history of queerness on screen, and we can’t wait to watch it with you as part of Queer Film Theory 101.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Frances Ha
show.headline: Greta Gerwig follows her dwindling dreams while navigating NYC

event.title: <show-title>Frances Ha</show-title>
event.headline: Gerwig dances through her early 20s in the best coming of age film of the 2010s
event.description:
2012 was the age of Millennial Ennui – Lena Dunham’s Girls was a think piece darling, you couldn’t go anywhere without hearing “Somebody That I Used to Know” by Gotye, and Greta Gerwig (with the help of future husband Noah Baumbach) was making a splash with her French New Wave-inspired critical darling FRANCES HA.

Shot entirely in black and white, FRANCES HA follows aspiring dancer Frances (Gerwig) as she tumbles awkwardly through her early 20s in New York City. She has trouble keeping up with friends, constantly feels low on cash, and career setbacks feel like monumental blows. She successfully taps into the universal feeling everyone gets as a young adult: ambitious yet aimless. With a knock-out script and performance from Gerwig, FRANCES HA is the blueprint for her future as an incredibly thoughtful coming-of-age filmmaker.

event.title: <show-title>Hello Mary Lou: Prom Night II</show-title>
event.headline: Mary Lou is dying to party
event.description: When Mary Lou's boyfriend lights her on fire at prom, her spirit is caught in a pirate trunk. Thirty years later, the trunk is opened and Mary Lou is resurrected as a body-possessing death machine with one thing on her mind: VIOLENCE!! HELLO MARY LOU: PROM NIGHT II conclusively proves that prom night doesn't have to be filled with SUV limos, unwanted acne, and broken dreams. It can also be filled with exploding Bibles, death via Apple IIe computer, and Michael Ironside. Beautifully photographed and perfectly paced, this underrated melding of CARRIE and A NIGHTMARE ON ELM STREET feels like an episode of Beverly Hills: 90210 as directed by Sam Raimi. It's that good. (Joseph A. Ziemba)

show.title: The Suckling
show.headline: ... all he wanted was a cuddle

event.title: <show-title>The Suckling</show-title>
event.headline: ...all he wanted was a cuddle
event.description:
THE SUCKLING has everything you love: exploding heads, severed hands, a fully articulated beastie, major problems with major appliances, and spinning dildos. The monster effects are a feat, but the cinematography and script are just as strong. There are characters you love to hate and hate to love. This film is designed to offend. It's about abortions and contains sexual assault. But like the best exploitation films, THE SUCKLING is deceptively intelligent and dances nimbly around both sides of a charged issue. It's exploitation and horror at its finest. (Annie Choi)

Note: This film includes a brief scene of a non-consensual sexual act.

DCP courtesy of Vinegar Syndrome and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: White of the Eye
show.headline: The only difference between a hunter and a killer... is his prey

event.title: <show-title>White of the Eye</show-title>
event.headline: A cut above the rest
event.description:
If you're a wealthy, attractive woman, stay out of Arizona, because you are fair game. A twisted killer is on the loose, who tortures and dissects his beautiful victims as part of a primitive Native American ritual. All the clues lead to one man who is clearly innocent. But nothing is as simple as that.The man, a sound expert, is targeted as the prime suspect in connection to f brutal murders of local suburban housewives, all who were attacked and mutilated in their homes. As he desperately tries to prove his innocence, his wife starts to uncover startling truths. David Keith and Cathy Moriarty star in this shocking mystery-thriller co-written and directed by Donald Cammell, director of DEMON SEED and co-director of PERFORMANCE. The film also features an eclectic score by Nick Mason (Pink Floyd) and Rick Fen. Lost amongst the rubble of mid to late 80's slashers, what emerges is less your typical HALLOWEEN-knockoff but a razor sharp underlining of the the hippie dream erosion - the last fruits of Americana rotting off the vine in the Arizona sun.

DCP courtesy of Shout! Factory and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: By Design
show.headline: A woman envies the life of a perfect chair so much that she swaps bodies with it

event.title: <show-title>By Design</show-title>
event.headline: A woman envies the life of a perfect chair so much that she swaps bodies with it
event.description: Upon seeing a gorgeous chair in a showroom, Camille (Juliette Lewis) realizes that she truly envies the life of this perfect piece of furniture. If only she could be someone’s favorite thing. When she and the chair swap forms, Camille learns that she is better liked as an inanimate object than she was as a person: her mother (Betty Buckley) finds her to be a better listener, and her best friends (Samantha Mathis and Robin Tunney) enjoy a newfound, friction-free rapport. As the chair, Camille is unable to speak or move, and winds up in the hands of Olivier (Mamoudou Athie), a minimalist bachelor who gradually becomes romantically fixated on his elegant new possession. Narrated by Melanie Griffith, this modern fable celebrates the messiness of female interiority with deadpan humor, surrealist flourishes, and interpretative dance numbers. An absurdist spin on the body-swap comedy, BY DESIGN is a bold new work from singular filmmaker Amanda Kramer.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
